ROC captures short‑term price acceleration while MACD confirms the direction of the underlying trend; combining them filters out false spikes, and using ATR‑scaled stops adapts to volatility, improving risk‑adjusted returns on a 15‑minute APT/USDC pair.

Adding an ADX trend strength filter ensures we only take ROC‑driven entries when the market is in a clear trend, reducing false signals in choppy periods; requiring a MACD line‑cross confirms direction and filters out isolated ROC spikes; an ATR volatility filter prevents entries during low‑volatility consolidation and provides dynamic stop‑loss/take‑profit levels, together improving risk‑adjusted returns.
